steps.title: Renewable and Non-Renewable Energy
steps.content:
**Non-renewable** sources (coal, oil, gas, nuclear) will eventually run out and most produce CO₂.

**Renewable** sources will not run out:

| Source | How it works |
|---|---|
| Solar | Panels convert light → electricity |
| Wind | Turbines convert KE of wind → electricity |
| Hydroelectric | Falling water drives turbines |
| Tidal | Tidal movement drives turbines |
| Geothermal | Heat from Earth drives steam turbines |
| Biomass | Burning organic material releases stored energy |

Renewables have **lower carbon emissions** but can be **intermittent** (sun/wind not always available).

steps.title: Energy Sources
steps.question: Which of these is a renewable energy source?
steps.correctFeedback: Correct! Wind energy is renewable — wind will not run out. Coal, natural gas, and nuclear fuel are non-renewable (finite supply).
steps.misconceptionFeedback: Renewable = will not run out in human timescales. Wind, solar, tidal, hydroelectric, geothermal, and biomass are renewable. Coal, gas, oil, and nuclear fuels are non-renewable — they take millions of years to form and are being used much faster.
